message.subject: New Research Inquiry from Emmanuel S. Delloro Jr
message.text:
New Research Inquiry

Contact Information:
Name: Emmanuel S. Delloro Jr
Email: emmanuel.delloro@msunaawan.edu.ph
Affiliation: College of Fisheries and Marine Sciences- Mindanao State University at Naawan
Designation: Faculty

Service Type: research


Project Background: We want to process DNA barcoding of fish juveniles. Juvenile fish are observed here at Northern Mindanao but it was not yet properly documented. In line with this, our project will do the morphological identification and confirmation through DNA barcoding. If possible we want to process from DNA extraction until Sequencing. 

Project Budget: 125000





Inquiry ID: leTHqki8HvoSm7KAH9hm
Submitted: 3/3/2026, 6:45:54 AM

Project Background: We want to process DNA barcoding of fish juveniles. Juvenile fish are observed here at Northern Mindanao but it was not yet properly documented. In line with this, our project will do the morphological identification and confirmation through DNA barcoding. If possible we want to process from DNA extraction until Sequencing.

Project Budget: 125000

Affiliation: Science Department, Mindanao State University - General Santos

Designation: Student

Service Details

Service Type: laboratory

Species: microbe-prokaryote

Research Overview: Our study focuses on the comparative analysis of bat gut microbiota in beach forest ecosystems. Specifically, we aim to: 1. Assess bat species composition and diversity within selected sites; 2. Characterize and compare gut microbiota composition across bat species using 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing; 3. Interpret potential indicators of bat health based on microbiome profiles; and 4. Explore correlations among feeding guild, diet, and microbiota composition in relation to food resource availability. We have already completed DNA extraction from fecal samples and are currently at the stage of DNA quantification and quality assessment.

Sample Count: 2

Workflow Type: Individual Assay

Individual Assay Details: 16s rRNA Amplicon Sequencing

message.subject: New Laboratory Inquiry from Vladimir Amadeus C. Mercado
message.text:
New Laboratory Inquiry

Contact Information:
Name: Vladimir Amadeus C. Mercado
Email: vcmercado@up.edu.ph
Affiliation: Division of Biological Sciences - UPV CAS
Designation: Student

Service Type: Laboratory
Species: Plant (Nervilia sp. leaf)

Sample Count: 3

Workflow: Individual Assay


Research Overview: The paper generally aims to investigate the taxonomy of Nervilia species observed in the University of the Philippines Visayas Miagao Campus. Specifically, it seeks to:

(1) describe the morphology of Nervilia species observed in the UP Visayas Miagao Campus using descriptive terminology and measurements;
(2) identify Nervilia species in the campus following the morphological species concept through plant examinations and measurement, comparison with herbarium sheets, and published descriptions, including protologues;
(3) apply DNA barcoding methods to generate preliminary molecular data and compare these with reference sequences from online databases to support or refine species identification; and
(4) investigate the habitat preferences, phenology, and associated species of Nervilia populations observed within the UP Visayas Miagao Campus.




Individual Assay Details: DNA Extraction











Inquiry ID: As06jMD2cmflxlXyQR8t
Submitted: 3/15/2026, 2:40:33 PM

Research Overview:

The paper generally aims to investigate the taxonomy of Nervilia species observed in the University of the Philippines Visayas Miagao Campus. Specifically, it seeks to: (1) describe the morphology of Nervilia species observed in the UP Visayas Miagao Campus using descriptive terminology and measurements; (2) identify Nervilia species in the campus following the morphological species concept through plant examinations and measurement, comparison with herbarium sheets, and published descriptions, including protologues; (3) apply DNA barcoding methods to generate preliminary molecular data and compare these with reference sequences from online databases to support or refine species identification; and (4) investigate the habitat preferences, phenology, and associated species of Nervilia populations observed within the UP Visayas Miagao Campus.

message.text:
New Laboratory Inquiry

Email: jatenmagbanua@gmail.com

Designation: Manager

Inquiry ID: vhw2ll8EdizqVl3NUUFP
Submitted: 3/3/2026, 7:23:33 AM
message.html:
New Laboratory Inquiry

Contact Information

Name: Justine Magbanua

Email: jatenmagbanua@gmail.com

Affiliation: Talarak Foundation Inc.

Designation: Manager

Service Details

Service Type: laboratory

Species: other (Fungus (Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis))

Research Overview: The project aims to determine the presence of (Bd) in populations of P. spelaeus in NI and to establish sentinel populations for P. spelaeus which will serve as a basis for any change to the frog populations over time thus giving a reference point for future studies and long term monitoring for the species. This project builds upon previous studies that have established the presence of (Bd) in Luzon and Mindanao populations of frogs. This poses an immediate danger to frog populations in NI, especially the endemic P. spelaeus.. As Bd spreads quickly, it is therefore necessary that the researchers investigate for possible cases in the local populations in order to come up with immediate plans to mitigate and control its spread. Surveys will be performed in selected sites across South-western Negros in previous study sites. Skin swabs will be taken from the animals and this will be analysed using real time PCR and the data collected will be used to know if Bd is indeed present in populations of this frog across the different study sites. The data will also be used to map distribution ranges of local populations. The establishment of a methodology for assessing population size in this species (consisting of transects around cave mouths, capture-mark-recapture techniques, and use of visual implant elastomer as a method of marking), as well as the use of these methods to generate a first robust population estimate, will enable work going forward to assess how the population is faring and quickly allow for the identification of any chytrid-related population crashes, as have occurred in other amphibian species. Objective 3: Determine presence of Bd in populations of P. speleaus 1.1 Collect samples from selected known populations of P. speleaus 1.2 Conduct tests (qPCR) to determine presence and prevalence of Bd on specimens from test site Objective 4: Construct a distribution map for Bd occurrence in Negros Island 1.1 Collect coordinates for each survey area 1.2 Map locations of occurrence in GIS software

Sample Count: 100

Workflow Type: Individual Assay

Individual Assay Details: PCR, If positive samples then qPCR for prevalence

Inquiry ID: vhw2ll8EdizqVl3NUUFP

Submitted: 3/3/2026, 7:23:33 AM

message.subject: New Laboratory Inquiry from Diomer Serra
message.text:
New Laboratory Inquiry

Contact Information:
Name: Diomer Serra
Email: serra.diomer@clsu2.edu.ph
Affiliation: Central Luzon State University
Designation: MS Biology Student

Service Type: laboratory








Inquiry ID: t7PX7bgt1wzKVZGi4DNj
Submitted: 3/9/2026, 6:02:17 AM
message.html:
New Laboratory Inquiry

Name: Diomer Serra

Email: serra.diomer@clsu2.edu.ph

Affiliation: Central Luzon State University

Designation: MS Biology Student

Service Details

Service Type: laboratory
Species: microbe-prokaryote
Sample Count: 1
Workflow: Individual Assay

Research Overview:

1. What are the specific objectives of the study? The primary objective of this study is to screen and quantitatively determine gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) production by gut-associated bacterial isolates from the honeybee (Apis mellifera) using a targeted LC-MS/MS approach. Specifically, the study aims to (i) induce GABA production through glutamate supplementation, and (ii) confirm and quantify extracellular GABA in bacterial culture supernatant. 2. How did you prepare your samples? Please include sample buffers, clean-up steps, enrichment steps, etc. Bacterial isolates will be cultured in MRS broth supplemented with monosodium glutamate (MSG) as a precursor for GABA production. After the induction period, culture will be centrifuged at 4,000 rpm for 10 min at 4 °C to remove bacterial cells. The resulting supernatants will be filtered through sterile 0.22 μm regenerated cellulose syringe filters to obtain cell-free supernatants. 3. Do you have an estimated concentration of your samples? Based on published studies using similar bacterial induction conditions and targeted LC-MS/MS workflows, extracellular GABA concentrations are expected to fall within the low micromolar to high micromolar range (approximately 1–500 μM). The applied 1:1000 dilution strategy is designed to ensure that final analyte concentrations fall within the linear dynamic range of the LC-MS/MS method. Exact concentrations will be determined using external calibration curves generated from authentic GABA standards. 4. Do you have references that you used to determine that LC-MS is best suitable for your objectives? Yes. The use of LC-MS/MS for this study is supported by published workflows demonstrating its applicability for detecting and quantifying amino acid–related metabolites produced during microbial fermentation. Casertano et al. (2024) employed targeted LC-MS/MS using a triple quadrupole mass spectrometer to quantify gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) and glutamate from bacterial culture supernatants, following defined chromatographic separation and external calibration, confirming the suitability of LC-MS/MS for targeted analysis of these metabolites. 5. Metabolite identification and database considerations The study focuses on targeted quantification of known metabolites, specifically GABA (and glutamate, where applicable), rather than untargeted metabolite discovery. Both analytes are well-characterized, low-molecular-weight metabolites that are expected to be present in mzCloud and other standard MS/MS libraries.

Review in Admin Panel

Inquiry ID: t7PX7bgt1wzKVZGi4DNj

Submitted: 3/9/2026, 6:02:17 AM

message.text:
New Laboratory Inquiry

Contact Information:
Name: HONIELYN N/A TAKA
Email: honielyn.taka@msunaawan.edu.ph
Affiliation: DEPARTMENT OF FISHERIES SCIENCE, COLLEGE OF FISHERIES AND MARINE SCIENCES, MINDANAO STATE UNIVERSITY AT NAAWAN
Designation: INSTRUCTOR, PROJECT LEADER

Service Type: Laboratory
Species: Animal (Crab)

Sample Count: 30

Workflow: Complete molecular workflow with Bioinformatics Analysis

Bioinformatics Analysis: Phylogenetics (1 Marker)

Research Overview: Mud crabs (Scylla spp.) are economically important crustaceans in the Indo-Pacific and play a significant role in fisheries and aquaculture in the Philippines. However, limited information exists on the genetic diversity and species composition of Scylla populations in Northern Mindanao. Because the species are morphologically similar and often misidentified, molecular techniques such as mitochondrial DNA sequencing are necessary for accurate species identification and genetic assessment, as well as for detecting possible hybridization among species.

This study aims to generate baseline genomic data on Scylla populations from selected coastal areas in Northern Mindanao through DNA extraction, gene amplification, sequencing, and bioinformatics analysis. The results will identify the Scylla species present, assess their genetic diversity, and examine potential hybrid occurrences and genetic relationships among populations. The findings will support sustainable fisheries management, aquaculture development, and conservation strategies for mud crab resources in the Philippines.

General Objective
The study aims to assess the genetic diversity and species composition of Scylla populations in Northern Mindanao using molecular and genomic approaches.

Specific Objectives
1. To collect mud crab (Scylla spp.) samples from selected coastal municipalities in Northern Mindanao to represent the regional population distribution.
2. To extract genomic DNA from collected crab specimens and prepare samples for molecular analysis.
3. To amplify and sequence target genetic markers suitable for species identification and genetic diversity assessment.
4. To confirm species identity of the collected samples using molecular identification methods such as sequence comparison and database matching.
5. To evaluate the genetic diversity and variation among Scylla populations across sampling sites using sequence analysis and comparative genomic data.
6. To generate baseline genetic information on Scylla populations in Northern Mindanao that can support sustainable fisheries management, conservation planning, and broodstock development programs.

message.html:
New Laboratory Inquiry

Name: Brittany Miroslava O. Briton

Email: bobriton@up.edu.ph

Affiliation: Institute of Fish Processing Technology - UPV CFOS

Designation: Graduate Student

Service Details

Service Type: laboratory
Species: plant
Sample Count: 24
Workflow: Individual Assay

Research Overview:

My study entitled, "Multi-omics investigation of the metabolome profile and surface microbiome of green tide-forming Ulva spp." seeks to explore the biomass utilization potential of green tide–forming Ulva spp. by investigating their metabolite landscape using non-targeted metabolomics and evaluating the composition and predictive functional potential of their associated microbiome using 16S rRNA metabarcoding. Green tides caused by species of the genus Ulva represent both an ecological challenge and a potentially valuable marine biomass resource which remains to be underexplored and underutilized. By profiling the chemical diversity of bloom-forming Ulva, this research seeks to identify a broad range of metabolites, including compounds that may have nutraceutical or industrial relevance. Although bioactivities will not be experimentally validated in this study, the resulting metabolite inventory will provide a foundation for future targeted functional and bioactivity investigations. In parallel, the study will characterize the structure of the Ulva-associated bacterial community and infer its predicted functional potential using bioinformatics tools. This will help identify microbial taxa and metabolic pathways that may contribute to secondary metabolite production. By integrating metabolomic and microbiome datasets, my study aims to uncover associations between specific metabolites and microbial community patterns, generating insights into host–microbe metabolic interactions and possible influence on biomass value.

Review in Admin Panel

Inquiry ID: J4h1PokgpSw1zkXyauvl

Submitted: 3/5/2026, 2:20:56 AM
